title: narcissus nevadensis subsp. nevadensis
intro: Narcissus nevadensis subsp. nevadensis is a type of daffodil native to the Sierra Nevada mountains in California and Nevada. It is a perennial plant with white or yellow trumpet-shaped flowers and long, strap-like leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. In herbal preparations, Narcissus nevadensis subsp. nevadensis is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and is also valued for its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues.
